project.name: airport-digital-twin
project.description: A real-time airport operations platform built on Databricks, combining physics-based flight simulation, live ADS-B tracking, and ML-powered predictions served through interactive 2D/3D visualization across any airport worldwide. Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

nodes.summary: Provides a mockable clock abstraction for the simulation, allowing tests to inject deterministic time functions via set_clock/reset_clock.

nodes.name: _constants.py
nodes.summary: Immutable constants for the synthetic flight system including airline turnaround speed factors, aircraft performance parameters (VREF speeds, descent profiles), wake turbulence categories, and turnaround phase dependencies.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure data module with no functions or classes — all exports are module-level typed dictionaries and tuples serving as lookup tables.

nodes.name: _event_buffers.py
nodes.summary: In-memory event buffer system that collects phase transitions, gate events, predictions, and turnaround events during simulation ticks, then drains them in batches for downstream consumption.

nodes.name: emit_phase_transition
nodes.summary: Records a flight phase transition event (e.g. approaching→landing) into the buffer with timestamp, coordinates, and diagnostic logging.

nodes.name: emit_turnaround_event
nodes.summary: Records a turnaround sub-phase event (deboarding, fueling, catering, etc.) for a parked aircraft into the turnaround buffer.

nodes.name: _flight_lifecycle_original.py
nodes.summary: Frozen original version of the flight lifecycle state machine preserved as a characterization test baseline. Contains the complete flight creation and state update logic before refactoring into smaller phase-specific update functions.
nodes.languageNotes: This file must never be modified — it serves as a regression reference per project rules. The monolithic _update_flight_state function (1255 lines) handles all flight phases in a single function.

nodes.name: _create_new_flight
nodes.summary: Creates a new FlightState for any flight phase (approaching, parked, departing, etc.) with appropriate initial position, waypoints, gate assignment, and turnaround schedule.

nodes.name: _update_flight_state
nodes.summary: Monolithic state machine advancing a flight through all phases (approach, landing, taxi, parked, pushback, takeoff, departure, enroute) with physics-based position updates, waypoint navigation, and phase transitions.

nodes.name: _flight_lifecycle.py
nodes.summary: Refactored flight lifecycle state machine with phase-specific update functions (_update_approaching, _update_landing, _update_taxi_to_gate, etc.) for improved readability and maintainability over the original monolithic version.
nodes.languageNotes: Each flight phase is handled by a dedicated function (~50-200 lines each), making the state transitions explicit and testable individually.

nodes.name: _create_new_flight
nodes.summary: Creates a new FlightState with support for FLIFO-seeded flights (registration, terminal, belt, scheduled times) and preferred gate assignment in addition to synthetic generation.

nodes.name: _update_approaching
nodes.summary: Advances an aircraft through the approach phase: waypoint navigation, altitude descent profile interpolation, speed deceleration, approach separation checks, and transition to landing or go-around.

nodes.name: _update_enroute
nodes.summary: Updates enroute/cruising aircraft with hemispheric altitude rules, STAR procedure assignment, and transition to approach phase when within airport range.

nodes.name: _update_taxi_to_gate
nodes.summary: Moves a landed aircraft along its taxi route from runway to assigned gate, handling waypoint navigation, speed factors, separation checks, and gate arrival transition.

nodes.name: _update_parked
nodes.summary: Manages aircraft turnaround at gate: progresses through turnaround sub-phases (deboarding, fueling, catering, boarding) and triggers pushback when complete.

nodes.name: _update_landing
nodes.summary: Handles the landing roll phase from touchdown to runway exit, decelerating the aircraft and transitioning to taxi-to-gate with route computation.

nodes.name: _update_departing
nodes.summary: Climbs a departing aircraft through SID waypoints toward cruise altitude, accelerating and transitioning to enroute phase once clear of the terminal area.

nodes.name: _update_takeoff
nodes.summary: Simulates takeoff roll sub-phases (lineup, roll, rotate, liftoff, initial climb) with aircraft-type-specific V-speeds and acceleration profiles.

nodes.name: _update_taxi_to_runway
nodes.summary: Navigates a departing aircraft along its taxi route from gate to departure runway, with speed management and departure queue hold logic.

nodes.name: _execute_go_around
nodes.summary: Initiates a go-around maneuver when landing is not possible (runway occupied, separation violation), setting climb targets and re-sequencing the aircraft for another approach.

nodes.name: _update_flight_state
nodes.summary: Dispatcher function that routes flight state updates to the appropriate phase-specific handler based on the flight's current phase.

nodes.name: _generation.py
nodes.summary: Flight generation and trajectory engine that spawns synthetic flights to meet target counts, manages flight lifecycle culling, and generates detailed position trajectories for visualization including approach, ground, departure, and go-around paths.
nodes.languageNotes: Trajectory functions produce dense position arrays by stepping through the flight lifecycle at sub-second intervals, used for the 3D visualization replay feature.

nodes.name: generate_synthetic_flights
nodes.summary: Main entry point that generates or updates synthetic flights to match target count, culling stale flights and spawning new ones using calibration profiles and schedule queues.

nodes.name: generate_synthetic_trajectory
nodes.summary: Generates a detailed position trajectory for a specific flight by determining its current phase and delegating to the appropriate trajectory builder (approach, ground, departure, or go-around).

nodes.name: _trajectory_approach
nodes.summary: Builds a dense approach trajectory by stepping through the flight lifecycle, producing lat/lon/alt/heading points every sub-second for smooth 3D visualization of the descent path.

nodes.name: _trajectory_ground
nodes.summary: Generates ground trajectory points for taxiing aircraft (both arrival and departure), stepping through taxi waypoints with realistic ground speed and heading changes.

nodes.name: _spawn_flights_to_target
nodes.summary: Spawns new flights (arrivals, departures, parked) to reach the target active count, using calibration profiles for traffic mix ratios and airport-specific airline/route selection.

nodes.name: get_flights_as_schedule
nodes.summary: Converts current active flight states into a FIDS-style schedule format with computed status, scheduled/estimated times, and flight type classification.

nodes.name: reset_synthetic_state
nodes.summary: Resets all simulation state (flight states, gate states, runway states, calibration, caches) for airport switching or test isolation.

nodes.name: _geo.py
nodes.summary: Low-level geographic utility functions for the simulation including heading calculation, distance computation (degrees/nm/meters), point-on-circle projection, position interpolation, and polygon containment checks.

nodes.name: _calculate_heading
nodes.summary: Computes the bearing (0-360 degrees) between two lat/lon positions using the spherical law of cosines formula.

nodes.name: _point_on_circle
nodes.summary: Projects a point at a given bearing and radius from a center position, used for computing approach waypoints and spawn positions.

nodes.name: _compute_gate_standoff
nodes.summary: Calculates minimum distance from a point to a line segment in meters, used for taxi route clearance checks.

nodes.name: _runway_ops.py
nodes.summary: Runway and gate operations manager handling runway occupancy, wake turbulence separation, gate assignment/release, taxi separation checks, and approach queue sequencing for the flight simulation.

nodes.name: _find_available_gate
nodes.summary: Finds the next available gate for an arriving aircraft, considering occupancy status, cooldown timers, and overflow stands when all primary gates are full.

nodes.name: _taxi_speed_factor
nodes.summary: Computes a dynamic speed multiplier for taxiing aircraft based on proximity to other aircraft, intersection conflicts, and approach to gate/runway, enforcing realistic ground movement speeds.

nodes.name: _check_approach_separation
nodes.summary: Validates minimum wake turbulence separation between approaching aircraft, slowing or holding trailing aircraft when spacing is insufficient.

nodes.name: _is_runway_clear
nodes.summary: Checks whether a runway is clear for landing or takeoff by verifying no aircraft currently occupies it and minimum time separation from last operation.

nodes.name: _schedule_queue.py
nodes.summary: Schedule-driven flight spawning queue that pulls from the pre-generated daily schedule to spawn flights at their scheduled times, maintaining separate arrival and departure queues with refresh logic.

nodes.name: ScheduleQueue
nodes.summary: Priority queue that feeds scheduled flights into the simulation at their scheduled times, with periodic refresh from the schedule generator and phase-determination logic for spawning.

nodes.name: _state.py
nodes.summary: Core shared mutable state definitions for the synthetic flight system including FlightPhase enum, FlightState dataclass with 40+ fields, runway/gate state containers, and the flight state dictionary with callsign tracking.
nodes.languageNotes: FlightState uses Python dataclasses with extensive optional fields covering both simulation state (waypoints, phase progress) and FLIFO ground-truth metadata (registration, terminal, belt).

nodes.name: FlightPhase
nodes.summary: Enum defining the nine operational phases of a flight from APPROACHING through ENROUTE, representing the complete lifecycle state machine.

nodes.name: FlightState
nodes.summary: Comprehensive dataclass holding all mutable state for a synthetic flight including position, velocity, phase, waypoints, turnaround schedule, and FLIFO metadata.

nodes.name: _taxi_routing.py
nodes.summary: Taxi route computation engine that generates realistic ground paths between runways and gates using taxiway spine geometry, terminal-aware routing, smooth turn interpolation, and pushback heading calculation.
nodes.languageNotes: Routes are computed as arrays of (lon, lat) waypoints using a synthetic taxiway spine derived from runway-to-terminal geometry, with Bezier-like smoothing at sharp turns.

nodes.name: _get_taxi_waypoints_arrival
nodes.summary: Computes the taxi route from runway exit to gate, projecting positions onto the taxiway spine and building a smoothed waypoint sequence.

nodes.name: _get_taxi_waypoints_departure
nodes.summary: Computes the taxi route from gate to departure runway threshold via the taxiway spine with turn smoothing.

nodes.name: _get_parked_heading
nodes.summary: Determines the nose-in parking heading for a gate position by analyzing terminal building geometry, apron edges, and nearby taxiway orientation.

nodes.name: _compute_gate_standoff
nodes.summary: Calculates the standoff distance from a gate reference point based on terminal proximity and aircraft size, preventing wing-tip conflicts.

nodes.name: airport_table.py
nodes.summary: Static airport reference data table containing ~1440 airports with IATA/ICAO codes, coordinates, country, and city information, plus lookup and proximity search functions.
nodes.languageNotes: The bulk of this file is a large dictionary literal (~1440 entries) with airport metadata. Only the last ~80 lines contain actual logic for lookups and haversine distance calculations.

nodes.name: find_nearby
nodes.summary: Finds airports within a specified distance range of a given airport using haversine formula, supporting configurable min/max radius.

nodes.name: baggage_generator.py
nodes.summary: Synthetic baggage handling simulation generating realistic bag events, misconnection probabilities, flight-level stats, alerts, and BHS (Baggage Handling System) throughput modeling with queue depth and jam simulation.

nodes.name: generate_bags_for_flight
nodes.summary: Generates individual bag records for a flight with realistic timing, status progression, passenger names, and connection/misconnection modeling.

nodes.name: simulate_bhs_throughput
nodes.summary: Simulates baggage handling system throughput over a time window, modeling injection point capacity, conveyor jams, queue depths, and processing times.

nodes.name: baggage_writer.py
nodes.summary: Writes baggage event records to a Databricks Unity Catalog Delta table using Spark SQL INSERT statements.

nodes.name: callsign_reconciler.py
nodes.summary: Converts between IATA flight numbers and ICAO callsigns using a comprehensive airline code mapping table, with normalization and flight identity comparison.

nodes.name: to_iata
nodes.summary: Converts an ICAO callsign (e.g., 'UAL123') to IATA format (e.g., 'UA123') using the airline mapping table.

nodes.name: circuit_breaker.py
nodes.summary: Circuit breaker pattern implementation for external API calls (OpenSky, FLIFO) that trips after consecutive failures and resets after a cooldown period.

nodes.name: APICircuitBreaker
nodes.summary: Tracks consecutive API failures and provides a can_execute() check that returns false when the failure threshold is exceeded, with automatic reset after a configurable cooldown.

nodes.name: databricks_job.py
nodes.summary: Entry point for running the flight data polling job on Databricks, configuring the poll loop with Databricks workspace SDK integration and Volume paths.

nodes.name: run_poll_job
nodes.summary: Configures and launches the continuous polling loop for flight data ingestion on Databricks, setting up workspace SDK, Volume output paths, and bbox parameters.

nodes.name: fallback.py
nodes.summary: Airport geometry provider managing airport center coordinates, gate positions (from OSM or generated defaults), terminal mappings, and coordinate offset calculations for multi-airport support.

nodes.name: get_gates
nodes.summary: Returns gate positions for the current airport, sourcing from OSM airport config service with fallback to generated default gates arranged around the airport center.

nodes.name: apply_airport_offset
nodes.summary: Computes and applies a coordinate offset to transform flight positions from one airport's coordinate space to another during airport switching.

nodes.name: flifo_client.py
nodes.summary: HTTP client for the SITA FLIFO (Flight Information) API, handling OAuth token acquisition, rate limiting, and querying flight data by airport or flight number.

nodes.name: FlifoClient
nodes.summary: SITA FLIFO REST API client with OAuth2 token management, automatic token refresh, and methods for airport-level and flight-number queries.

nodes.name: flifo_mapper.py
nodes.summary: Transforms raw FLIFO API responses into the internal flight record format used by the simulation, extracting delays, gate assignments, times, and codeshare information.

nodes.name: map_flifo_record
nodes.summary: Maps a single FLIFO API record to the internal flight dictionary format, computing delay minutes, extracting gate/terminal/belt, and normalizing status codes.

nodes.name: opensky_client.py
nodes.summary: HTTP client for the OpenSky Network REST API providing real-time aircraft state vectors within a geographic bounding box, with token-based authentication and rate limit handling.

nodes.name: OpenSkyClient
nodes.summary: OpenSky Network API client with token authentication, configurable base URL, and bounding-box state vector queries returning raw ADS-B data.

nodes.name: poll_job.py
nodes.summary: Continuous polling loop that fetches flight data (from OpenSky or synthetic generator), writes results to a landing path, and uses a circuit breaker for fault tolerance.

nodes.name: poll_and_write
nodes.summary: Runs an infinite polling loop fetching flight state data at configurable intervals, writing JSON to the landing path with circuit breaker protection against API failures.

nodes.name: schedule_generator.py
nodes.summary: Generates realistic daily airport flight schedules with proper airline/route/aircraft selection based on calibration profiles, traffic patterns, gate assignment with occupancy tracking, and configurable delay distributions.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses extensive calibration profile data for per-airport airline distributions, route shares, and traffic patterns to generate statistically realistic schedules.

nodes.name: generate_daily_schedule
nodes.summary: Generates a complete daily flight schedule for an airport with hour-by-hour traffic volume, airline selection, route assignment, gate allocation, and delay injection based on calibration profiles.

nodes.name: get_nearby_airports
nodes.summary: Finds airports near a reference point using the airport table's haversine search plus fallback lookup, with caching for repeated calls.

nodes.name: get_cached_schedule
nodes.summary: Returns the cached daily schedule for an airport, regenerating it if stale or missing, with thread-safe cache invalidation.

nodes.name: weather_generator.py
nodes.summary: Generates realistic aviation weather products (METAR and TAF) with proper formatting, diurnal temperature/wind/visibility patterns, cloud layer generation, and flight category classification (VFR/MVFR/IFR/LIFR).

nodes.name: generate_metar
nodes.summary: Generates a properly formatted METAR observation string with wind, visibility, clouds, temperature, and altimeter data varying by time of day.

nodes.name: generate_taf
nodes.summary: Generates a Terminal Area Forecast (TAF) with validity period, base conditions, and weather change groups (TEMPO/BECMG).

nodes.name: weather_types.py
nodes.summary: Defines the shared WeatherSnapshot Pydantic model used as a contract between the synthetic weather generator and real METAR parser, ensuring both produce structurally consistent weather data.

nodes.name: WeatherSnapshot
nodes.summary: Pydantic BaseModel representing a standard weather observation with wind, visibility, temperature, and flight category fields required by all weather data sources.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package init for the ML module that re-exports core prediction APIs (FeatureSet, extract_features, DelayPrediction, DelayPredictor, predict_delay) for convenient top-level imports.

nodes.name: acdm_adapter.py
nodes.summary: Converts Airport Collaborative Decision Making (A-CDM) operational records into TurnaroundFeatureSet instances and training targets, bridging real-world airport data with the ML training pipeline.

nodes.name: acdm_record_to_features
nodes.summary: Transforms a single A-CDM record dict into a TurnaroundFeatureSet and target turnaround duration by extracting timestamps, computing differences, and classifying aircraft characteristics.

nodes.name: convert_acdm_dataset
nodes.summary: Batch-converts a list of A-CDM records into parallel lists of TurnaroundFeatureSet instances and target values, filtering out records with missing or invalid data.

nodes.name: congestion_model.py
nodes.summary: Implements airport congestion prediction by modeling areas (runways, taxiways, aprons, terminals) from OSM config and computing congestion levels based on flight density, capacity, and time-of-day scaling.

nodes.name: CongestionPredictor
nodes.summary: Main congestion prediction engine that builds airport area definitions from OSM config and predicts congestion levels, wait times, and bottlenecks for each area based on current flight positions.

nodes.name: predict_congestion
nodes.summary: Module-level convenience function that creates a CongestionPredictor and returns area congestion predictions for a list of flights.

nodes.name: delay_model.py
nodes.summary: Provides flight delay prediction using a rule-based model with airport-profile calibration, outputting estimated delay minutes, probability, category, and contributing factors.

nodes.name: DelayPredictor
nodes.summary: Rule-based delay predictor calibrated with airport profile data that produces delay estimates with probabilities, categories (minor/moderate/severe/critical), and contributing factor analysis.

nodes.name: features.py
nodes.summary: Core feature engineering module that extracts ML-ready features from flight dictionaries including altitude categorization, distance binning, heading quadrants, and array serialization for model input.

nodes.name: FeatureSet
nodes.summary: Dataclass holding extracted flight features (altitude, speed, heading, distance, time-based signals, weather) used as the standard input format for ML prediction models.

nodes.name: extract_features
nodes.summary: Extracts a FeatureSet from a raw flight data dictionary by computing derived fields like altitude category, distance bins, heading quadrant, and time-of-day signals.

nodes.name: gate_model.py
nodes.summary: Intelligent gate assignment system that scores and recommends optimal gates based on aircraft size, airline affiliation, international status, taxi distance, and current gate occupancy loaded from OSM data.

nodes.name: GateRecommender
nodes.summary: Multi-criteria gate recommendation engine that loads gate data from OSM, scores candidates on size compatibility, airline preference, proximity, and availability, then returns ranked recommendations with explanations.

nodes.name: Gate
nodes.summary: Data model representing an airport gate with properties (size, terminal, operator, coordinates) and a factory classmethod to construct from OSM gate node data.

nodes.name: gse_model.py
nodes.summary: Models Ground Support Equipment (GSE) operations including turnaround timing, equipment requirements by aircraft category, fleet status tracking, depot placement, and travel time estimation.

nodes.name: get_gse_requirements
nodes.summary: Returns the list of required GSE types and quantities for a given aircraft category (narrow-body, wide-body, regional), including fuel trucks, baggage loaders, catering vehicles, and pushback tugs.

nodes.name: get_turnaround_timing
nodes.summary: Computes phase-by-phase turnaround timing (passenger deplane/board, fueling, catering, cargo, cleaning) with durations scaled by aircraft category.

nodes.name: calculate_turnaround_status
nodes.summary: Determines the current status of each GSE phase for a flight based on elapsed time since gate arrival, returning progress percentages and active/completed states.

nodes.name: generate_gse_positions
nodes.summary: Generates realistic 2D positions for GSE vehicles around a gate based on equipment type and approach direction, used for map visualization.

nodes.name: get_fleet_status
nodes.summary: Computes the airport-wide GSE fleet utilization summary showing available/in-use/maintenance counts per equipment type based on current gate operations.

nodes.name: get_depot_locations
nodes.summary: Generates GSE depot positions distributed around the airport perimeter based on airport center coordinates and gate count.

nodes.name: obt_features.py
nodes.summary: Defines feature sets for Off-Block Time prediction (OBTFeatureSet and OBTCoarseFeatureSet) and provides training data extraction from simulation recordings with timestamp-derived features.

nodes.name: OBTFeatureSet
nodes.summary: Dataclass capturing detailed features for OBT prediction including aircraft class, gate type, boarding progress, weather conditions, and congestion metrics.

nodes.name: extract_obt_training_data
nodes.summary: Extracts OBT training samples from simulation recording files by parsing gate arrival/departure timestamps and computing features at different prediction horizons.

nodes.name: obt_model.py
nodes.summary: Off-Block Time prediction model with three predictor variants (fine-grained OBTPredictor, coarse OBTCoarsePredictor, two-stage TwoStageOBTPredictor) supporting CatBoost and sklearn backends with conformal quantile regression for prediction intervals.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses conformal quantile regression (CQR) for calibrated prediction intervals, with CatBoost as the preferred backend and scikit-learn GradientBoosting as fallback.

nodes.name: OBTPredictor
nodes.summary: Full-featured OBT predictor using gradient boosting with quantile regression, supporting train/predict/save/load lifecycle and calibrated confidence intervals via conformal prediction.

nodes.name: TwoStageOBTPredictor
nodes.summary: Two-stage OBT predictor that combines a coarse estimate at schedule time with a refined prediction at parking time for improved accuracy as departure approaches.

nodes.name: registry.py
nodes.summary: Per-airport ML model registry that lazily instantiates and caches model instances (delay, gate, congestion, turnaround, OBT) keyed by ICAO code, loading trained models from UC Volume pickles when available on Databricks.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses lazy imports for heavy dependencies (scikit-learn, CatBoost) to keep the lightweight Databricks App runtime fast when those models are not needed.

nodes.name: AirportModelRegistry
nodes.summary: Caches ML model instances per airport ICAO code, creating calibrated models on first access using AirportProfileLoader and optionally loading trained pickles from UC Volume.

nodes.name: get_model_registry
nodes.summary: Module-level singleton accessor that returns the shared AirportModelRegistry instance, creating it on first call.

nodes.name: training.py
nodes.summary: MLflow training orchestration for delay, turnaround, and OBT models with experiment tracking, metric logging, model artifact persistence to UC Volumes, and optional Unity Catalog registration.

nodes.name: train_delay_model
nodes.summary: Trains the delay prediction model with MLflow experiment tracking, logs metrics and parameters, and saves model artifacts to a specified output path or UC Volume.

nodes.name: train_turnaround_model
nodes.summary: Trains the turnaround prediction model on simulation-generated feature sets, logging training metrics and saving the trained model pickle.

nodes.name: train_obt_model
nodes.summary: Trains the OBT prediction model with optional conformal calibration, saving artifacts and logging metrics to MLflow.

nodes.name: transfer_learning.py
nodes.summary: Transfer learning pipeline that fine-tunes a simulation-trained turnaround model on real A-CDM operational data using lower learning rates, bridging the sim-to-real gap for production predictions.

nodes.name: fine_tune_turnaround
nodes.summary: Fine-tunes a pre-trained turnaround model on real A-CDM data by loading the base model, converting A-CDM records to features, and retraining with a lower learning rate for domain adaptation.

nodes.name: turnaround_features.py
nodes.summary: Comprehensive turnaround feature engineering with three feature set granularities (coarse, detailed, boarding-phase), plus training data extraction from simulation recordings with weather, congestion, and operational context features.
nodes.languageNotes: Defines three progressively detailed dataclasses (TurnaroundCoarseFeatureSet, TurnaroundFeatureSet, TurnaroundBoardFeatureSet) supporting multi-stage prediction where each stage refines the estimate.

nodes.name: TurnaroundFeatureSet
nodes.summary: Detailed turnaround feature set including aircraft class, gate characteristics, weather, congestion, airline hub status, and temporal signals, with conversion methods to coarse and boarding-phase variants.

nodes.name: extract_training_data
nodes.summary: Extracts turnaround training samples from a simulation JSON file by parsing flight gate events, computing time-based features, and classifying aircraft and route characteristics.

nodes.name: extract_training_data_from_recording
nodes.summary: Extracts turnaround training data from live simulation recordings (as opposed to post-hoc JSON), handling timestamp parsing and feature computation from real-time state snapshots.

nodes.name: turnaround_model.py
nodes.summary: Turnaround time prediction with five model classes (TurnaroundPredictor, TurnaroundCoarsePredictor, TwoStageTurnaroundPredictor, TurnaroundBoardPredictor) using gradient boosting with conformal quantile regression for calibrated pushback time prediction intervals.
nodes.languageNotes: Mirrors the OBT model architecture with CatBoost/sklearn dual backend and conformal quantile regression, adapted for turnaround-specific features and multi-stage prediction timing.

nodes.name: TurnaroundPredictor
nodes.summary: Primary turnaround time predictor using gradient boosting with quantile outputs and conformal calibration, supporting full train/predict/save/load lifecycle with feature importance extraction.

nodes.name: TwoStageTurnaroundPredictor
nodes.summary: Two-stage turnaround predictor combining a coarse estimate at T-90 minutes with a refined prediction at parking time, progressively narrowing the confidence interval as departure approaches.

nodes.name: TurnaroundBoardPredictor
nodes.summary: Specialized predictor for the boarding phase of turnaround, providing refined pushback estimates once boarding has commenced using boarding-specific features.

nodes.name: __init__.py

nodes.name: detector.py
nodes.summary: YOLO-based aircraft detection model that identifies aircraft bounding boxes in satellite imagery and generates corresponding masks for inpainting.

nodes.name: AircraftDetector
nodes.summary: Wraps a YOLO model for aircraft detection in satellite tiles, providing detect() for bounding boxes and generate_mask() for binary masks suitable for inpainting.

nodes.name: InpaintingPipeline
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the detect-then-inpaint workflow: runs AircraftDetector to find aircraft, generates masks, applies LaMaInpainter, and returns the cleaned image with detection metadata.

nodes.name: serving.py
nodes.summary: MLflow PythonModel wrapper for serving the inpainting pipeline as a Databricks Model Serving endpoint, handling model loading and predict API for base64-encoded image inputs.

nodes.name: AircraftInpaintingModel
nodes.summary: MLflow PythonModel that loads the InpaintingPipeline in load_context and serves aircraft removal predictions via a predict method accepting base64 image payloads.

nodes.name: AirportRepository

nodes.name: get_airport_repository

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README to understand what this project does: an interactive airport digital twin deployed on Databricks that simulates real-time flight operations with synthetic data, ML predictions, and 2D/3D visualization. The README covers the full feature set (7 ML models, WebSocket protocol, DLT pipelines, PWA mobile experience) and establishes the vocabulary used throughout the codebase. Start with the README to understand what this project does: an interactive airport digital twin deployed on Databricks that simulates real-time flight operations with synthetic data, ML predictions, and 2D/3D visualization. The README covers the full feature set (7 ML models, WebSocket protocol, DLT pipelines, PWA mobile experience) and establishes the vocabulary used throughout the codebase.

tour.title: Backend Entry Point
tour.description: The FastAPI application entry point bootstraps the entire backend: it registers all API routers, initializes services (Lakebase connections, airport config, weather, ML models, data generators) via background tasks during startup, and serves the compiled React frontend as static assets. This file reveals the full dependency tree of the server. FastAPI's lifespan context manager (async generator with yield) replaces the older on_event('startup') pattern. Code before yield runs on startup; code after runs on shutdown. Background tasks during lifespan allow non-blocking initialization of slow services like database connections.

tour.title: Data Models and Schemas
tour.description: The type system defines the domain vocabulary shared between backend and frontend. The Python schemas define FlightPhase (a 9-state enum), FlightPosition, and FlightStatus using Pydantic. The TypeScript types mirror these with Flight, DelayPrediction, GateRecommendation, and CongestionArea interfaces. These contracts are the backbone that every service and component consumes. Pydantic v2 models use Python type annotations with field validators for runtime data validation. The TypeScript counterparts use interface declarations which provide compile-time type safety but no runtime enforcement -- the frontend applies Number() coercion for coordinate fields that arrive as strings from the API.

tour.title: Simulation Engine
tour.description: The core simulation engine orchestrates synthetic flight lifecycles: it builds schedules from calibration profiles, spawns flights, advances them through phase transitions (approach, landing, taxi, gate, departure), handles capacity constraints, go-arounds, diversions, and weather events. The engine drives the entire demo mode and is the primary data source when no live ADS-B feed is available. The engine uses a time-stepped simulation loop (not event-driven) where each tick advances all active flights by a fixed dt. This approach trades some efficiency for predictability -- every flight gets updated every frame, making debugging and replay straightforward.

tour.title: Flight Lifecycle State Machine
tour.description: The flight lifecycle module implements the state machine that governs each individual flight from spawn to termination. Phase-specific update functions (_update_approaching, _update_landing, _update_taxi_to_gate, etc.) handle position interpolation, altitude curves, and transition triggers. This is the most complex single file in the codebase, calling into runway operations, taxi routing, event buffers, and geo utilities. The state machine uses a dispatch dictionary mapping FlightPhase enum values to handler functions rather than a large if/elif chain. Each handler returns the flight's updated state, and phase transitions are triggered by condition checks (altitude thresholds, distance to waypoint) rather than timers.

tour.title: Airport Data from OpenStreetMap
tour.description: All airport geometry (runways, gates, taxiways, terminals) is dynamically fetched from OpenStreetMap via the Overpass API -- never hardcoded. The OSM parser constructs queries, handles caching and retries, and the converter transforms raw nodes/ways into the internal airport config format with proper geo-to-local coordinate transformation. The airport config service acts as the singleton orchestrator that imports from multiple sources and builds taxiway graphs. The Overpass QL query language uses element type filters (node, way, relation) with tag selectors in brackets. The parser constructs queries like [aeroway=runway](area.airport) to fetch all runways within an airport boundary polygon.

tour.title: Calibration System
tour.description: Per-airport calibration profiles provide the statistical realism that makes simulations believable. Each profile contains airline market shares, route distributions, fleet mix, delay patterns, and hourly traffic shapes. The multi-tier loader tries UC Volume first, then local JSON files, then known hand-researched profiles for 43 major airports, and finally falls back to regional templates based on continent. The AirportProfileLoader implements a chain-of-responsibility pattern where each loading tier (UC Volume, local file, known profiles, OpenFlights, hardcoded) is tried in order. If a tier fails or returns incomplete data, the next tier supplements it. This graceful degradation ensures the simulation always has enough data to run.

tour.title: ML Model Suite
tour.description: The ML layer provides predictions that enhance the digital twin beyond raw simulation: delay prediction uses rule-based models calibrated to airport profiles, gate assignment scores candidates by aircraft size and airline affiliation, and congestion detection monitors runway/taxiway utilization. The model registry lazily instantiates per-airport model instances, loading trained pickles from UC Volumes on Databricks. The registry uses lazy instantiation with airport ICAO codes as cache keys. Models are only created when first requested for a given airport, avoiding startup cost for airports that may never be viewed. The pattern is similar to a factory combined with a flyweight cache.

tour.title: Lakebase Data Layer
tour.description: Lakebase (managed PostgreSQL on Databricks) serves as the low-latency operational data store, providing sub-10ms reads for real-time flight positions, weather, schedules, and baggage status. The service provides connection pooling with OAuth, read replicas, and CRUD operations for 14 domain tables. Eight other services import it, making it the most depended-upon backend component. The Lakebase schema uses PostgreSQL's ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE (upsert) extensively since flight positions are continuously overwritten. Composite indexes on (icao24, last_contact) enable efficient time-range queries without full table scans.

tour.title: Real-Time Data Flow
tour.description: The WebSocket endpoint broadcasts flight positions to connected clients using delta-based updates -- only changed fields are sent after the initial full snapshot. The frontend useFlights hook maintains flight state via WebSocket with automatic reconnection and exponential backoff, while FlightContext aggregates simulation data, live feeds, and predictions into a unified state consumed by all visualization components. Delta-based WebSocket updates send only fields that changed since the last frame, dramatically reducing bandwidth. The client merges deltas into its local state using object spread, and React's useMemo ensures downstream components only re-render when their specific data slice changes.

tour.title: Frontend Visualization
tour.description: The frontend provides two synchronized visualization modes. The 2D view uses Leaflet with custom markers rotated to heading, trajectory lines with Ramer-Douglas-Peucker simplification, and airport infrastructure overlays. The 3D view uses Three.js with GLTF aircraft models, extruded terminal buildings from OSM polygons, satellite imagery ground textures, and orbital camera controls. A viewport sync hook keeps both views aligned. React Three Fiber (@react-three/fiber) wraps Three.js in React's component model -- 3D objects become JSX elements with props for position, rotation, and material. The useFrame hook provides per-frame animation callbacks equivalent to requestAnimationFrame, but integrated with React's render lifecycle.

tour.title: DLT Pipeline (Medallion Architecture)
tour.description: Delta Live Tables pipelines process flight data through the medallion architecture. Bronze ingests raw flight status from Lakebase, silver explodes state vectors into typed columns with data cleansing, and gold aggregates by icao24 into latest-state summaries with derived phase classification. A parallel baggage pipeline processes bag events through the same three tiers. DLT uses Python decorators (@dlt.table) to declare pipeline nodes declaratively. Each function returns a DataFrame transformation, and DLT infers the DAG from read dependencies. The STREAMING keyword enables incremental processing -- only new rows are processed on each pipeline run.

tour.title: Deployment Infrastructure
tour.description: Deployment is fully automated via Databricks Asset Bundles. The deploy.sh script orchestrates frontend build, DABs deploy (which provisions the app, volumes, jobs, and pipelines), Unity Catalog table creation via SQL API, app stop/start cycling, and service principal permission grants. The databricks.yml defines three targets (dev, prod, free) with per-environment variables. CI/CD workflows run tests on every push and deploy on merge to main. Databricks Asset Bundles (DABs) use a declarative YAML config to define infrastructure-as-code for Databricks resources. The bundle deploy command computes a diff against the remote state and applies only the changes, similar to Terraform's plan/apply model but specialized for the Databricks platform.
